conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who's 13 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask: Do you live in the UK, and is Alex living with you or are you the main person responsible for them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, that's the thing - I don't actually live in the UK at the moment. Alex does live with me, but we're abroad. Also, Alex's other parent lives with us too and they already claim Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for explaining your situation. I have a few follow-up questions to understand this better.

First, can you tell me which country you're currently living in with Alex?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

I need to ask a few more questions to assess your eligibility properly:

Are you or Alex's other parent (who is already claiming Child Benefit) working in the UK or paying UK National Insurance contributions? Or do either of you work for a UK-based employer or UK government organization?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us is working in the UK or paying UK National Insurance. We don't work for any UK-based employers or UK government organizations either.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information.

Just to clarify one more important point: You mentioned that Alex's other parent already claims Child Benefit for Alex. Is this Child Benefit being claimed and paid by the UK system currently, or was it a claim from when you lived in the UK previously?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're claiming it from the UK system currently - they've been receiving the payments.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Eligibility Decision

Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

**For Alex (age 13):**
- **Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child at any time.** Since Alex's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex through the UK system, you cannot make a separate or additional claim for the same child.
- Additionally, you are currently living in Spain and do not have the necessary UK connection required to claim Child Benefit while living abroad. Generally, to claim while living outside the UK, you would need to be working in the UK, paying UK National Insurance contributions, working for a UK-based employer or government organization, or be covered under certain international agreements - none of which apply in your case.

If circumstances change in the future (for example, if the other parent stops claiming, or if you return to live in the UK), you may wish to reassess your eligibility at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is not eligible for Child Benefit in this case for two main reasons: 1) Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child at any given time. Alex's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex through the UK system, which means you cannot make a separate claim for the same child. 2) You are living in Spain and are not working in the UK, paying UK National Insurance contributions, or working for a UK-based employer or government organization. Generally, to claim Child Benefit while living abroad, you need to have a sufficient connection to the UK through work or National Insurance contributions, or be covered under certain international agreements. Since Alex's other parent already has an active claim and you lack the necessary UK connection while living abroad, you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is living in Spain with their 13-year-old child Alex. Alex's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex through the UK system. The claimant is not eligible because only one person can claim Child Benefit per child at any time, and additionally, the claimant does not have the necessary UK connection (through work or National Insurance contributions) required to claim while living abroad.

Generally, to claim Child Benefit while living abroad, you need to have a sufficient connection to the UK through work or National Insurance contributions, or be covered under certain international agreements. Since Alex's other parent already has an active claim and you lack the necessary UK connection while living abroad, you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
